findings.description: The knowledge file describes command-line option columns as "カラム `paramA=valueA`、`paramB=valueB` を追加すると", presenting `paramA=valueA` as if it were the column specification. In the source, the column name is `paramA` and its value is `valueA` — these are separate things. The notation conflates column name and value into a single token, which could mislead an AI into thinking the column name itself includes `=valueA`.
findings.source_evidence: Source shows a table where columns are named `paramA` and `paramB` with values `valueA` and `valueB` respectively. The source says adding columns named `paramA` (with value `valueA`) and `paramB` (with value `valueB`) results in the option `-paramA=valueA -paramB=valueB`.
